content: The most obvious outcome of the reproductive process is the generation of individuals of similar design, but in sexual reproduction, they may not be exactly alike. The resemblances as well as differences are marked. The rules of heredity determine the process by which traits are reliably inherited. Many experiments have been done to study the rules of inheritance. (i) Why is an offspring of a human being not a true copy of its parents in sexual reproduction? (ii) While performing experiments on inheritance in plants, what is the difference between F1 and F2 generations? (iii) (A) Why do we say that variations are useful for the survival of a species over time? OR (B) Study Mendel's cross between two plants with a pair of contrasting characters (RRYY x rryy). He observed 4 types of combinations in the F2 generation. Which of these were new combinations? Why do new features not present in the parents appear in the F2 generation?
subject: Science

content: The ability of a medium to refract light is expressed in terms of its optical density. Optical density has a definite connotation and is not the same as mass density. On comparing two media, the one with the higher refractive index is optically denser. The other medium with a lower refractive index is optically rarer. The speed of light through a given medium is inversely proportional to its optical density. (i) Determine the speed of light in diamond if the refractive index of diamond with respect to vacuum is 2.42. (ii) Refractive indices of glass, water, and carbon disulphide are 1.5, 1.33, and 1.62, respectively. If a ray of light is incident in these media at the same angle, write the increasing order of the angle of refraction. (iii) (A) The speed of light in glass is 2x10^8 m/s and in water is 2.25x10^8 m/s. (a) Which one is optically denser and why? (b) A ray of light is incident normally at the water-glass interface. What will happen to the path of the ray after entering the glass? Give a reason. OR (B) The absolute refractive indices of water and glass are 4/3 and 3/2, respectively. If the speed of light in glass is 2x10^8 m/s, find the speed of light in (i) vacuum and (ii) water.
subject: Science

content: The ability of a medium to refract light is expressed in terms of its optical density. Optical density has a definite connotation; it is not the same as mass density. On comparing two media, the one with the higher refractive index is optically denser than the other. The medium with a lower refractive index is optically rarer. The speed of light through a given medium is inversely proportional to its optical density. (i) Determine the speed of light in diamond if the refractive index of diamond with respect to vacuum is 2.42. (Speed of light in vacuum = 3x10^8 m/s). (ii) Refractive indices of glass, water, and carbon disulphide are 1.5, 1.33, and 1.62, respectively. If a ray of light is incident in these media at the same angle, write the increasing order of the angle of refraction. (iii) (A) The speed of light in glass is 2x10^8 m/s and in water is 2.25x10^8 m/s. (a) Which of these is optically denser and why? (b) A ray of light is incident normally at the water-glass interface. What will happen to the path of the ray after entering the glass? Give reasons. OR (iii) (B) The absolute refractive indices of water and glass are 4/3 and 3/2, respectively. If the speed of light in glass is 2x10^8 m/s, find the speed of light in (i) vacuum and (ii) water.
subject: Science

content: In animals, control and coordination are provided by nervous and muscular tissues. The nervous system consists of an organized network of neurons. In humans, thinking is a complex activity involving neural connections concentrated in the brain, which acts as the main coordinating center. The brain and spinal cord constitute the Central Nervous System, which receives and integrates information from the body. (a) How is the brain protected from shocks and injuries? (b) Write the main functions of (i) sensory neurons and (ii) motor neurons in a reflex arc. (c) Which part of the brain is involved in (i) picking up a pencil and (ii) vomiting? State whether these actions are voluntary or involuntary. OR (c) How does the central nervous system communicate with other parts of the body? Name two components of this system.
subject: Science

content: When a ray of light moving in one medium enters another medium obliquely, it deviates from its path. This phenomenon is called refraction of light. The ability of a medium to refract light is expressed in terms of optical density, which is distinct from mass density. We use the terms 'optically rarer' and 'optically denser' to describe media. If medium A is optically denser than medium B, it implies that the refractive index of A is greater than that of B. Light slows down when traveling from a rarer to a denser medium and bends towards the normal.
subject: Science

content: When a ray of light moving in one medium enters another medium obliquely, it deviates from its path. This phenomenon is called refraction of light. (b) Absolute refractive indices of water and glass are 4/3 and 3/2 respectively. (i) In which medium is the speed of light greater? (ii) If a ray of light enters obliquely from glass to water, will it bend towards or away from the normal? (c) If the speed of light in glass is 2 x 10^8 m/s, find the speed of light in (i) water and (ii) vacuum. OR (c) Explain why a light ray emerging from a rectangular glass slab is parallel to the incident ray. Draw a diagram to justify this.
subject: Science
